target.title: feat(cron): main session announce delivery and Feishu/outbound fixes
target.body:
## Purpose and scenarios

1. **Main session cron delivering to Feishu/Telegram etc.**  
   Allow scheduled jobs to run in the main session (with memory) and deliver the result to a configured channel (e.g. Feishu) instead of only supporting webhook. Upstream currently clears main's announce delivery config.

2. **Cron plain-text direct delivery to avoid 60s timeout**  
   Plain-text results going through announce hit the main session lane and often cause gateway timeout; use direct outbound delivery when a resolved target exists.

3. **Feishu union_id support**  
   When the session stores `on_` (union_id), the code was sending with `user_id` and Feishu API returned 400. Recognize `on_` and use `receive_id_type=union_id`.

4. **Docker media path rewrite**  
   When running in a container with host volume mount, tools may return host paths; support `OPENCLAW_HOST_STATE_DIR` to rewrite them to the container state dir.

---

## Changes

### 1) Main session cron announce delivery
- **src/cron/service/jobs.ts**: Only clear main's `delivery` when mode is neither `webhook` nor `announce`; allow `sessionTarget === 'main'` in `assertDeliverySupport`.
- **src/cron/service/timer.ts**: When main runs and `job.delivery.mode === 'announce'` with `channel`, pass `job.delivery` as the heartbeat override to `runHeartbeatOnce` so the run result is sent to the configured channel/to.
- **src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-add.ts**: For main + systemEvent, accept `--channel` / `--to` (or `--announce`) and set delivery in params.
- **src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts**: Update test so switching to main keeps announce delivery.

### 2) Cron plain-text direct delivery
- **src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts**: When there is a resolved delivery target and the payload is text-only, use direct `deliverOutboundPayloads` instead of the announce path to avoid lane queue and 60s timeout.

### 3) Feishu union_id
- **extensions/feishu/src/targets.ts**: Add `on_` prefix and `union_id:` handling; `resolveReceiveIdType` returns `union_id` for `on_`; update `normalizeFeishuTarget`, `formatFeishuTarget`, `looksLikeFeishuId`, `detectIdType`.
- **extensions/feishu/src/targets.test.ts**: Add test for `on_` → `union_id`.

### 4) Docker media path
- **src/web/media.ts**: When `OPENCLAW_HOST_STATE_DIR` is set and the media URL is an absolute path under that dir, rewrite it to the current state dir path for container access.

---

## How to verify

- Main + Feishu: `openclaw cron add --name remind --session main --system-event "Remind user to take a break." --channel feishu --to "ou_xxx" --every 12h`, then `openclaw cron run <id>`; Feishu should receive the main session reply.
- Feishu union_id: When target is `on_xxx`, send message API uses `receive_id_type=union_id` and no longer returns 400.
- Plain-text direct: Isolated cron with text-only result should use direct delivery and avoid 60s timeout.

target.title: status: surface global tool profile hints in overview
target.body:
## Summary
- surface the configured global tool profile baseline in `openclaw status` overview output
- surface the configured global tool profile baseline in `openclaw status --all` overview output
- add a practical hint when the global `tools.profile` is set to `messaging`

## Why
A messaging-first setup can feel unexpectedly limited even when the configuration is valid. Surfacing the global tool profile baseline directly in status output makes that easier to diagnose without digging through config files, while staying explicit that per-agent and provider overrides may differ.

## Changes
- add a `Global tools profile` overview row to `openclaw status`
- add the same `Global tools profile` overview row to `openclaw status --all`
- include a practical hint for `tools.profile: "messaging"`
- include a short descriptive note for `tools.profile: "full"`
- keep the wording scoped to the global config baseline rather than the effective resolved runtime profile

## What did not change
- no runtime behavior
- no tool policy logic
- no config schema values
- no effective-profile resolution logic

## Change Type
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #39954
- Related #39963

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`openclaw status` and `openclaw status --all` now show the configured global tool profile baseline in the Overview section when one is set. For `messaging`, the output explains that it is intentionally narrow by design and points users toward `tools.profile: "full"` for a broader global command/control baseline. Per-agent and provider overrides may still differ.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification
### Environment

- Runtime/container: local clone
- Model/provider: n/a
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.profile`

### Steps
1. Set `tools.profile` in config (for example `messaging` or `full`)
2. Run `openclaw status`
3. Run `openclaw status --all`
4. Inspect the Overview section

### Expected
- status output surfaces the configured global tool profile baseline when one is set
- `messaging` output includes a practical hint that the global profile is intentionally narrow

### Actual
- updated status overview code now adds a `Global tools profile` row in both commands

## Evidence
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification
- Verified scenarios: reviewed the exact diff and confirmed the row is added to both status renderers; user verified the behavior in a local dev environment.
- Edge cases checked: no row is shown when no tool profile is set; other profiles still render plainly; wording stays scoped to the global config baseline rather than implying effective runtime resolution.
- What I did not personally verify: cross-environment behavior outside the user's local dev setup.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/status.command.ts`, `src/commands/status-all.ts`, `src/commands/status.tool-profile.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: misleading wording or overly verbose status output

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: maintainers may prefer the hint in a different status surface or wording style
- Mitigation: kept the change limited to overview rows in the two existing status commands without changing behavior and made the wording explicit about global scope

target.title: Doctor: structure sandbox repair checks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: after #83753 and #84290, sandbox registry/image repair and sandbox scope warnings were still partly legacy positional doctor workflows, so the structured lint/fix/dry-run contract did not cover this bucket end to end.
- Solution: convert sandbox registry files, sandbox images, and sandbox scope warnings into structured doctor health checks while preserving the existing doctor order and old non-repair behavior.
- Rigor/idempotency: the detector is now the read-only source for lint, dry-run, and repair verification; registry migration still uses the existing migration function; image repair distinguishes repairable default images from operator-managed custom images; scope warnings remain read-only.
- What did NOT change: this does not add public CLI flags, build Docker images by default, or make custom sandbox images implicitly repairable.

This PR is intentionally stacked on #83753 and #84290. The diff against `main` includes those earlier stages; the new sandbox stage starts at `75cfed4048 fix(doctor): structure sandbox repairs`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #83753
- Related #84290
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Continue the structured doctor conversion for the next config/update/doctor/migrate bucket. Sandbox checks had the same risk as the previous two PRs: legacy positional repair logic and structured health reporting could drift, especially around dry-run effects and custom image ownership.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Structured doctor repair conversion for sandbox registry files, sandbox image checks, and sandbox scope warnings with isomorphic `detect -> repair/dry-run -> detect` semantics and operator-managed custom image handling.
- Real environment tested: Local WSL source checkout plus direct AWS Crabbox lease `cbx_c86ebac61277` (`tidal-hermit`), run `run_1807262a6457`, OpenClaw source checkout synced from this branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-sandbox.ts src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts`; `git diff --check`; `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; Crabbox retry command `corepack enable && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`.
- Evidence after fix: Local format, diff, tsgo, focused tests, and autoreview passed. Crabbox run `run_1807262a6457` exited 0 with 5 test files / 66 tests passed. Proof artifact with image and run note: https://gist.github.com/giodl73-repo/c6ff5e2d3d3d5ca04e5fd3c5fb0c660d.
- Observed result after fix: structured dry-run reports sandbox registry migration effects without mutation; sandbox image dry-run reports repairable default image setup separately from operator-managed custom images; repair skips non-repairable custom images with actionable warnings; Docker-unavailable and non-Docker-browser backend cases remain warnings; sandbox scope findings stay read-only; contribution order remains after legacy cron and before gateway services.
- What was not tested: full changed gate, actual Docker image build/pull, package/update survivor lanes, and live Docker sandbox execution; this slice proves the structured doctor contract and focused sandbox repair behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): prior behavior kept this sandbox bucket on legacy positional repair/warning helpers instead of first-class structured health checks with matching dry-run/diff coverage.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the previous structured doctor stages had not yet converted the sandbox bucket, leaving registry file migration, image checks, and scope warnings outside the same structured repair contract.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no focused tests asserted custom sandbox images are operator-managed, default images are the only repairable build-script cases, or sandbox contribution ordering with structured dry-run repairs.
- Contributing context (if known): #83753 established the structured maintenance pattern and #84290 continued it for plugin install/registry repairs; this PR applies the same pattern to sandbox checks.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts`, `src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`, `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: structured lint/repair/dry-run behavior for sandbox registry files, sandbox images, and scope warnings.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these are doctor/config seam behaviors; focused tests cover detector, repair context, dry-run effects, and contribution ordering without requiring live Docker builds.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts` and conversion-plan tests cover the shared repair orchestration.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Doctor lint/fix can now report and dry-run sandbox registry, image, and scope findings through the structured health-check path while preserving existing repair order and prompts.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
doctor repair flow -> positional sandbox helpers -> limited structured dry-run proof

After:
doctor repair flow -> sandbox structured health checks -> positional order preserved -> repair/dry-run/diff proof from one contract
```

## Dry-run Diff / Effects Catalog

This PR separates sandbox repair effects from concrete diffs. Sandbox registry and Docker-image repairs are state/process work, so dry-run reports the effect that would occur and avoids inventing a file diff unless a real file/config before/after is available.

- `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files`
  - State effects: `kind: "state"`, actions for migrating legacy sandbox container/process registries into the current sharded state layout, targets the affected sandbox registry family, `dryRunSafe: true`.
  - No file diff is emitted for the sharded registry migration because the repair is a state migration plan, not a single stable config rewrite exposed by the health check.

- `core/doctor/sandbox/images`
  - Process effects: `kind: "process"`, actions for building or repairing missing/stale sandbox base/browser images, target the affected image, `dryRunSafe: false`.
  - Inspection/skip effects stay explicit when Docker is unavailable, custom images are configured, or operator-managed images are detected; these do not masquerade as config diffs.

- `core/doctor/sandbox-scope`
  - Read-only scope findings remain findings only. They do not report repair effects or diffs because no automatic mutation is available for that check.

## Repair Validation / Detect-After

Invariant for all real structured repairs in this bucket: a successful non-dry-run repair is not considered fixed from its effect record alone. The shared repair runner immediately re-runs the same health check, scoped to the original finding paths, and records remaining findings if the install, cleanup, rewrite, or state update is still detectable. Dry-run remains preview-only because no mutation is applied.

- Sandbox registry repairs call the sharded registry migration, then `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` detection must no longer report the legacy monolithic registry path. Lower-level migration tests also assert the legacy files and locks are gone and entries are readable from the sharded registry.
- Sandbox image repairs call the Docker image repair path for default repairable images, then `core/doctor/sandbox/images` detection must no longer report the missing image.
- Non-repairable sandbox image cases, such as Docker unavailable or custom operator-managed images, return `skipped`; skipped repairs are not counted as fixed and are intentionally not validated as repaired.

## Stack Refresh / Detect-After Test Placement

- Branch restacked on the updated plugin branch and pushed with sandbox-owned detect-after proof: `6d28631957 test(doctor): validate sandbox repair detection`.
- Bucket covered here: `core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` and `core/doctor/sandbox/images` detect-after validation; plugin/manifest detect-after coverage remains in the lower stacked PR.
- Exact focused proof after restack: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 3 files / 56 tests; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-sandbox.ts src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts` passed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL local checkout plus direct AWS Crabbox Linux VM proof
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0 in Crabbox, pnpm 11.1.0 after `corepack enable`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): doctor CLI/config/sandbox checks
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic sandbox config states for missing registry files, missing default images, custom image ownership, Docker-unavailable, non-Docker browser backend, and scope warnings

### Steps

1. Run focused sandbox doctor tests for helper detectors, core health checks, contribution order, conversion plan, and repair flow.
2. Run format, diff, tsgo, and autoreview locally.
3. Retry focused tests in direct AWS Crabbox after installing dependencies on the raw box.

### Expected

- Structured sandbox checks report stale/missing sandbox states, preserve legacy repair behavior, and keep custom images operator-managed.

### Actual

- Expected behavior observed in local tests and Crabbox run `run_1807262a6457`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local format, diff check, tsgo, focused tests, repeated autoreview, and Crabbox focused test proof.
- Edge cases checked: Docker unavailable warning; missing default base/browser images with build scripts; custom browser image has no default build script; custom image repair is skipped with operator guidance; registry file detector reports missing legacy files; scope warnings are read-only.
- What you did **not** verify: full changed gate, actual Docker image build/pull, package/update survivor lanes, or live Docker sandbox execution.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: structured conversion could drift from the old sandbox helper behavior.
  - Mitigation: helpers now expose reusable detectors/formatters and focused tests cover both structured health checks and legacy helper prompts.
- Risk: custom sandbox images could accidentally be treated as repairable by the default setup scripts.
  - Mitigation: detector and repair tests lock custom base/browser images to operator-managed warnings unless a default build script is known.





## Stack Refresh: Structured Sandbox Image Repair Notes

Rebased onto refreshed #83753 and #84290. This branch now uses the finalized note-phase contract for sandbox image repairs: converted `core/doctor/sandbox/images` calls the new structured `repairSandboxImages(...)` helper instead of legacy `maybeRepairSandboxImages(...)`, and missing-image operator guidance flows through `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` rather than direct `note(...)` calls. Legacy top-level doctor behavior remains on the old helper until that path is converted.

The sandbox image helper now returns structured `{ config, changes, warnings }` repair output, accepts already-detected issues from the health check, preserves repair-time prompting, and keeps dry-run/effects output concrete for buildable image repairs and operator-managed custom images.

Additional verification after restack:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-sandbox.ts src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 4 focused files / 46 tests passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed on head `f0615f42e5e886b14dd731b07e7fbd50c8d34d62`.


## Stack Refresh: Shared Prompter Presentation Path

Restacked onto refreshed #83753/#84290 and removed the sandbox-specific `repairNote` parameter. The structured sandbox image repair helper now uses the shared `DoctorPrompter.note(message, title?)` presentation capability, while legacy `maybeRepairSandboxImages(...)` remains the old print wrapper for unconverted doctor contribution paths.

Additional verification after restack:


commands.review_summary:

target.title: feat(config): use datetime suffix for backup rotation instead of numeric
target.body:
Replace numeric backup rotation (`.bak.1`, `.bak.2`, …) with datetime-stamped suffixes (`.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHmmss` in UTC) so users can identify known-good backups at a glance without checking file modification times.

Closes #39923

## Summary

- **`rotateConfigBackups()`** — renames `.bak` → `.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHmmss` instead of shifting numbered slots
- **`cleanOrphanBackups()`** — keeps the N-1 most recent datetime-stamped backups (lexicographic sort = chronological); removes legacy numbered files (`.bak.1`, `.bak.2`) and other orphans on first upgrade run
- **`hardenBackupPermissions()`** — discovers datetime-stamped files via `readdir` instead of iterating a fixed numeric range
- **Collision handling** — appends `-2`, `-3`, … suffix for sub-second collisions

target.title: fix(discord): remove invalid message_reference from webhook payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- Remove `message_reference` from Discord webhook execute payloads
- The [Execute Webhook](https://docs.discord.com/developers/resources/webhook) endpoint does not support this field; it is only valid on the [Create Message](https://docs.discord.com/developers/resources/message) endpoint (bot-token)
- The field was silently ignored by Discord, but sending unsupported fields risks future rejection

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify webhook sends still succeed without `message_reference`
- [ ] Confirm bot-token reply behavior (`replyTo` via `sendMessageDiscord`) is unaffected

target.title: Fix: preserve modelOverride in agent handler (#5369)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the intermittent race condition where sub-agent model overrides were silently ignored (~3% failure rate).

- When `sessions.patch` writes a fresh `modelOverride` between when the agent handler captures its local `entry` reference and when it builds `nextEntryPatch`, the captured stale value would clobber the fresh store value through `mergeSessionEntry({...freshStore, ...patch})`.
- The fix removes the stale `entry?.X` writebacks from `nextEntryPatch` (incl. `modelOverride`, `providerOverride`, `cliSessionIds`, `cliSessionBindings`, `claudeCliSessionId`, gated `pluginOwnerId`). `mergeSessionEntry` already preserves anything not in the patch, so fresh store values survive the write.
- Rotation-clearing for `sessionFile` (added by #79076) is preserved through a conditional spread that only writes `sessionFile: undefined` on session rotation.

## Root Cause

The agent handler was:
1. Reading the session entry early via `loadSessionEntry()` and holding it in a local variable.
2. Building `nextEntryPatch` with `modelOverride: entry?.modelOverride` and several siblings — values read from that captured local entry, not the fresh store.
3. Calling `updateSessionStore()` whose mutator does `mergeSessionEntry(store[primaryKey], nextEntryPatch)` — which spreads `{...freshStore, ...patch}`, so any stale value in the patch clobbers the fresh store value written concurrently (e.g., by `sessions.patch` setting `modelOverride` on a sub-agent between spawn and run).

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`: drop stale `entry?.X` writebacks from `nextEntryPatch`. Switch the patch type to `Partial<SessionEntry>`. Gate `pluginOwnerId` behind `entry === undefined`. Convert the `sessionFile` rotation-clearing from #79076 into a conditional spread that fires only when `entry.sessionId !== sessionId`.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`: add the original #5369 regression and a broader regression guard for the whole stale-writeback class (sendPolicy, skillsSnapshot, thinkingLevel, fastMode, verboseLevel, traceLevel, reasoningLevel, systemSent, spawnedWorkspaceDir, spawnDepth, cliSessionIds, cliSessionBindings, claudeCliSessionId).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When `sessions.patch` writes a fresh `modelOverride` between when the agent handler captures its local `entry` reference and when it builds `nextEntryPatch`, the captured stale value clobbers the fresh store value through `mergeSessionEntry({...freshStore, ...patch})`. Per #5369, this manifested as ~3% of sub-agent runs silently ignoring an explicit model override and falling back to the parent's model.

**Real environment tested:**
- Host: Linux 6.14.0-34-generic on x86_64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw production session-store modules from this branch (`src/config/sessions/store.ts`, `src/config/sessions/types.ts`).
- Multipass guest VM `ai-assistant`: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, IP 10.119.193.216, Node v22.22.2, same modules from this branch via a host mount at `/home/ubuntu/openclaw-pr5369`.

Both run the production `loadSessionStore` / `updateSessionStore` / `mergeSessionEntry` code paths against a real on-disk JSON store in a per-run tmp directory. No mocking. No shims.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
node --import tsx scripts/repro-5369.mts 1000
```

The driver lives in this branch (local-only, intentionally not committed). For each of 1000 iterations it:

1. Initializes a session entry on disk via `updateSessionStore` (no override yet).
2. Captures the entry via `loadSessionStore({ skipCache: true })` — the same shape the agent handler holds in its local `entry` variable.
3. Fires a concurrent `updateSessionStore` to set `modelOverride: haiku-4.5-iter-N` (mimicking what `sessions.patch` does during sub-agent setup).
4. Builds `nextEntryPatch` two ways and applies each through the same `mergeSessionEntry(store[primaryKey], nextEntryPatch)` call shape that `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` uses:
   - PRE-FIX shape: `modelOverride: capturedEntry.modelOverride` (writes back the captured stale `undefined`).
   - POST-FIX shape: `modelOverride` dropped from the patch — `mergeSessionEntry` preserves the fresh store value.
5. Reads the persisted entry back from disk and reports whether the override survived.

**Evidence after fix:**

Host (Linux 6.14, Node v22.22.0):

```
[repro #5369] node v22.22.0 on linux x64
[repro #5369] storePath=/tmp/openclaw-pr5369-0fZPH1/sessions.json
[repro #5369] iterations=1000

[repro #5369] running PRE-FIX patch shape (entry?.modelOverride writeback)...
[repro #5369] PRE-FIX :  1000/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (338 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=undefined

[repro #5369] running POST-FIX patch shape (modelOverride dropped from patch)...
[repro #5369] POST-FIX:  0/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (254 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0

[repro #5369] verdict: fix VERIFIED
```

Multipass guest VM `ai-assistant` (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Node v22.22.2):

```
[repro #5369] node v22.22.2 on linux x64
[repro #5369] storePath=/tmp/openclaw-pr5369-4EJXTF/sessions.json
[repro #5369] iterations=1000

[repro #5369] running PRE-FIX patch shape (entry?.modelOverride writeback)...
[repro #5369] PRE-FIX :  1000/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (2602 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=undefined

[repro #5369] running POST-FIX patch shape (modelOverride dropped from patch)...
[repro #5369] POST-FIX:  0/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (1337 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0

[repro #5369] verdict: fix VERIFIED
```

**Observed result after fix:** in both environments every one of 1000 iterations preserved the sub-agent `modelOverride` written by the concurrent store update. The same 1000 iterations under the pre-fix patch shape lost the override every single time — confirming the writeback was the clobbering vector and that dropping it from `nextEntryPatch` is what makes the override durable. The persisted on-disk values (`actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0`) match what `sessions.patch` wrote, end-to-end, through the production merge code.

The 100% pre-fix loss rate (vs the ~3% reported in #5369) reflects that the driver deterministically forces the stale-then-fresh ordering on every iteration. Production traffic only hits that ordering when `sessions.patch` lands in the narrow window between the agent handler's `loadSessionEntry` and its own `updateSessionStore` call — about 3% of the time per the linked issue. Whenever that ordering does occur in production, the driver shows the pre-fix shape always loses the override and the post-fix shape never does.

**What was not tested:** end-to-end Anthropic API calls burning credit on each of the 1000 iterations. The sub-agent dispatch loop is upstream of the `modelOverride` persistence the fix addresses; a real model loop would only confirm what the persisted `modelOverride` value is, which the driver already reads back from disk on every iteration. Driving 1000 real Anthropic calls would not provide additional information about the persistence behavior the fix changes.

Fixes #5369

Supersedes #8398 (rebased onto latest main to resolve conflicts).

target.title: fix(agents): recover invalid OpenAI Responses reasoning replay
target.body:
Fixes the OpenAI Responses half of #84002.

This intentionally does not change Slack main/thread session routing. The issue combines two continuation failures; this PR addresses the deterministic Responses replay failure where preserved encrypted reasoning can be rejected with `thinking_signature_invalid`.

## Root cause

OpenAI Responses can reject a replayed encrypted reasoning item when the stored `rs_*` content is no longer verifiable. Current history cleanup only drops replayable OpenAI reasoning when the model snapshot changes or when the reasoning block is orphaned, so a same-provider/same-model continuation can replay the stale encrypted reasoning again.

That error was also classified as a generic schema/format rejection, so terminal failures surfaced as "provider rejected the request schema" instead of a replay-state recovery error.

## What changed

- Classify `thinking_signature_invalid` and encrypted reasoning verification/decryption failures as replay-invalid.
- Wrap OpenAI Responses stream calls with a one-shot recovery path:
  - if the request fails before any streamed chunk, or the current Responses transport emits a pre-start assistant `error` event/result with invalid encrypted reasoning, drop replayable OpenAI reasoning blocks
  - downgrade paired `call|fc` tool call ids after dropping reasoning
  - retry the same model call once
  - keep dropping replayable reasoning for later calls in the same session wrapper after recovery is activated
- Do not retry if any stream chunk was already yielded, to avoid duplicate visible output/events.

## User impact

A Slack or channel continuation that hits stale OpenAI Responses encrypted reasoning should get one clean retry instead of ending the turn with no visible assistant content. If the retry still fails, the user-facing error now points to invalid session/replay state instead of a generic schema rejection.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI Responses continuation recovery for `thinking_signature_invalid` / invalid encrypted reasoning replay.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch (`edc7ccba2a0ab7a3f972362b5ee74753ff0fb302`) on macOS with Node `v24.15.0`. The committed proof harness uses the real OpenAI SDK HTTP transport and OpenClaw's current `createOpenAIResponsesTransportStreamFn()` plus `wrapOpenAIResponsesStreamWithReplayRecovery()` against a loopback `/v1/responses` endpoint. It does not mock the OpenAI SDK; the loopback endpoint returns provider-shaped HTTP 400 and SSE responses so reviewers can inspect the request/retry payload transition.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx scripts/proofs/openai-responses-replay-recovery.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
[openai-transport] [responses] error provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.4 name=Error status=400 code=thinking_signature_invalid type=invalid_request_error causeName=undefined causeCode=undefined message=400 The encrypted content for item rs_real_transport_proof could not be verified. Reason: Encrypted content could not be decrypted or parsed.
[agent/embedded] [session-recovery] OpenAI Responses reasoning replay rejected; retrying once without replayable reasoning: sessionId=real-http-transport-proof-session
OpenAI Responses replay recovery proof: PASS
Runtime path: real OpenAI SDK HTTP transport against loopback /v1/responses
Mocking: no vi.mock/openai SDK mock; loopback server returns provider-shaped HTTP/SSE
Requests observed: 2
Request paths: /v1/responses, /v1/responses
First response: HTTP 400 thinking_signature_invalid
First request input types: reasoning, message
Retry request input types: message
Retry dropped replayed reasoning: true
Retry preserved replayed message: true
Recovered assistant text: recovered via real http transport
Final stopReason: stop
```

Observed result after fix: The first real SDK HTTP request contains replayed `reasoning` and receives a provider-shaped `thinking_signature_invalid` 400. The wrapper retries once. The second real SDK HTTP request drops replayed `reasoning`, preserves the replayed visible `message`, consumes the SSE response, and returns assistant text with `stopReason: stop`.

Committed inspectable proof: `scripts/proofs/openai-responses-replay-recovery.ts` is the manual proof harness. `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/openai-responses-transport-replay-recovery.test.ts` remains the regression test that locks in the mocked-SDK transport error-event path.

What was not tested: Live Slack and live OpenAI Responses were not used. Slack session routing from #84002 is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```text
node --import tsx scripts/proofs/openai-responses-replay-recovery.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/proofs/openai-responses-replay-recovery.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/openai-responses-transport-replay-recovery.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

## Validation

```text
openai-responses-replay-recovery.ts proof harness: PASS
script oxlint: 0 warnings, 0 errors
openai-responses-transport-replay-recovery.test.ts: 2 test files, 2 tests passed
thinking.test.ts: 33 passed
pi-embedded-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts: 64 passed
tsgo core: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

target.title: Feature/exec denylist
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: This PR introduces a new feature - exec denylist. this is done to bridge the gap between unusable allowlist where the agent asks approval for every single command it runs and full on YOLO agent can do everything unrestricted. The feature sets some boundary for the agent so we don't have agent bypass things like web_search tool and run curl/wget instead and get prompt injected. - Problem: - Why it matters: safer than YOLO - What changed: added exec denylist feature - What did NOT change (scope boundary): other features ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior addressed: exec denylist blocks model-initiated shell network fetches (`curl`/`wget`) in a real gateway run, preventing a headless agent from bypassing safer fetch tooling. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway and Control UI exec approvals on macOS, running an Ollama `qwen3.6:35b-a3b-q4_K_M` scheduled cron agent with effective `tools.exec` security resolved to `denylist`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Configured exec approvals to use denylist, ran the scheduled `AI Lab Morning Brief` cron job, then inspected live gateway logs and the persisted runtime transcript with `openclaw logs --limit 300 --plain` and the matching session transcript. - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Screenshots above plus copied redacted live runtime output below. - Observed result after fix: Seven attempted `exec` commands containing `curl` were denied by rule `default-shell-network-fetch`; the model received `exec command is denied due to command in deny list`, then switched to `web_fetch` and completed the cron run instead of bypassing the safer fetch path. - What was not tested: content:notTested - This proof did not verify iMessage delivery; the cron delivery later timed out on the local iMessage bridge, unrelated to exec denylist behavior. It also did not test every possible custom deny rule shape beyond the default shell network-fetch rule. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Redacted runtime evidence: ```text 2026-05-17T11:15:14.803Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=f65c003a792cfdc0 length=100 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.804Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=16c1f70c7f7d90e6 length=655 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.805Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=10a2a034cfd89748 length=125 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.806Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=72a121ccd1eeb9f2 length=126 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.807Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=a0ea71b5b6367cd0 length=169 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.808Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=1b2a1a8991ec2015 length=123 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 2026-05-17T11:15:14.809Z info exec denylist: denied command hash=cc66320e783f16f7 length=527 host=gateway agent=main trigger=cron ruleIndex=0 redacted transcript: toolResult x7 -> exec command is denied due to command in deny list redacted transcript: assistant -> Exec is blocked on this run. Let me use web_fetch for the heavy lifting. cron result: finished status=ok durationMs=90606 model=qwen3.6:35b-a3b-q4_K_M provider=ollama ``` ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) Tests were written for this, but for validation so deny mode is synthetic ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Denies undesired exec runs List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). target.title: fix(gateway): deliver internal source replies to TUI
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: TUI-originated chat runs could miss message-tool source replies because the Gateway modeled WebChat, but not TUI, as an internal chat surface.
- Solution: Move internal chat surface identity into the Gateway protocol contract, use it for route inheritance, and broadcast message-tool source replies back over Gateway chat events.
- What changed: TUI/WebChat/Control UI/native UI-mode clients are handled through a shared internal-surface contract; current-session aliases are centralized; source-reply metadata is preserved through normalization/media/TTS; transcript mirrors are upgraded with safe fallback; sensitive source media stays live-only.
- What did NOT change: External channel delivery policy, provider auth, native non-UI route inheritance, and normal Pi assistant-turn persistence.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #38957
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TUI users should see the same visible source reply that the agent sends through the message tool. The previous route/source-surface split let the reply exist in transcript machinery, and in WebChat-oriented internal handling, while the active TUI chat stream could see no final reply.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: TUI-originated `chat.send` source replies no longer disappear from the active TUI when an agent uses the message tool for the visible source reply.
Real environment tested: Live local dev host with the managed systemd gateway stopped, no listener left on port `18789`, and a foreground gateway started from this worktree on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` at exact pushed PR head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8` (`OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (7b838cc)`). The live run used the real terminal TUI, the configured Codex harness with `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and the same local dev auth state. Earlier negative proof was run on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway.service
ps -ef | grep -E 'openclaw.*gateway|gateway --port|gateway run|tui' | grep -v grep || true
ss -ltnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':18789' || true
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact --force
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call health --json --timeout 10000
node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-proof-20260520-0136 --timeout-ms 180000 --message 'OPENCLAW_EXACT_HEAD_TUI_SOURCE_REPLY_7b838cc_0136 Use the message tool to send exactly this visible reply to the current chat: TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible. Do not include any other final answer text.'
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call chat.history --json --timeout 20000 --params '{"sessionKey":"live-proof-20260520-0136","limit":20}'
```
Because `tui --message` did not visibly dispatch before connect, I manually submitted the exact same prompt in the real TUI PTY. After collecting proof, I exited the TUI, stopped the foreground worktree gateway with SIGINT, and left `openclaw-gateway.service` inactive as allowed for this dev host.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the live exact-head TUI contained the rendered standalone reply in the active chat.
```text
TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.
```
`chat.history` for `live-proof-20260520-0136` showed session id `a06bbaa5-9077-4fa5-a034-8a6f5adf4ea0`, the user prompt nonce `OPENCLAW_EXACT_HEAD_TUI_SOURCE_REPLY_7b838cc_0136`, a `message` tool call with `{"action":"send","message":"TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible."}`, and a tool result reporting `"status":"ok"`, `"deliveryStatus":"sent"`, `"channel":"webchat"`, `"target":"current-run"`, `"sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"`, and `"sourceReplySink":"internal-ui"`. The durable `delivery-mirror` assistant message persisted the same text with idempotency key `bc81f146-0445-4312-bd41-5c8ba78dd0e4:internal-source-reply:0`.
Observed result after fix: The exact source reply sent through the live `message` tool appeared in the real TUI session and was also persisted as the internal source-reply mirror, proving the fixed Gateway source-reply event path reaches TUI instead of only transcript or another internal UI path.
What was not tested: Live external-channel delivery such as Telegram/Discord/Slack was not re-tested for this PR-head proof; this proof targets the reported TUI source-reply route. The explicit `tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789` path was not used because the URL override requires explicit credentials; the configured TUI/gateway path was used instead. The existing focused tests cover external route non-inheritance, native non-UI route inheritance, media mirrors, rich source replies, dropped managed outgoing image mirrors, and sensitive-media handling.
Before evidence: On exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`, I started a foreground base gateway with `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18789 --bind loopback --ws-log compact`, launched `node scripts/run-node.mjs tui --session live-negative-20260519-1331 --message 'Live negative proof request OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331...' --timeout-ms 180000`, then manually submitted the same prompt in the real TUI PTY because base `--message` did not visibly dispatch before connect. The live base TUI returned to `connected | idle` after the run and still showed only the submitted prompt, with no standalone `OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331` reply. Gateway history proved the tool did run: the model emitted `message` with `{"action":"send","message":"OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331"}`, the tool result reported `"deliveryStatus":"sent"`, `"sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"`, and `"sourceReplySink":"internal-ui"`, and a durable `delivery-mirror` assistant message was persisted with idempotency key `0bfe34e8-42ad-465e-a997-3a77d3dd53bc:internal-source-reply:0`. `sessions.list` reported `status=done`, `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `runtimeMs=12079`, `hasActiveRun=false`. That reproduces the reported failure shape: the source reply is sent/persisted, but the active TUI does not receive the visible reply before this patch.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Gateway route resolution and source-reply replay treated WebChat as the internal source surface but did not include the TUI client identity (`openclaw-tui`, mode `ui`).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No Gateway/TUI WebSocket test asserted that message-tool source replies are delivered to TUI clients.
- Contributing context: Source-reply metadata was lost by several normalization/media/TTS transformations, so rich/media source replies could not be reliably mirrored or replayed.

## Design Notes

- Internal chat surface identity now belongs to `src/gateway/protocol/client-info.ts`, the protocol-owned place where Gateway client ids are already defined.
- `src/utils/message-channel.ts` decides whether a client is an internal chat surface; it treats WebChat/TUI/Control UI/id-only legacy internal clients as internal, and only treats native app ids as internal when they are actually in `mode=ui`.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts` now owns source-reply broadcast/mirror behavior, including transcript mirror upgrades, final-event preparation, and the fallback that strips unreadable managed outgoing image URLs when a mirror was never attached to a transcript message.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts` owns assistant display-content projection, so `chat.ts` no longer carries the source-reply content munging inline.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` uses the shared internal-surface contract for route inheritance and source-reply broadcast, so internal chat surfaces do not accidentally inherit external delivery routes while native `mode=node` callers still can.
- Transcript mirror rewrite failures now fall back instead of being counted as successful upgrades, preventing stale or unsafe mirror content from being treated as repaired.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `src/utils/message-channel.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a TUI WebSocket client receives a final chat event for a message-tool internal source reply, including route inheritance boundaries, media, delayed mirrors, and sensitive-media mirror handling.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug sits at the Gateway/client event boundary and source-surface contract, not inside an external provider.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

TUI users now see message-tool source replies in the active chat stream. Sensitive media in source replies remains live-only and is not prewritten into durable transcript mirrors.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> durable mirror only -> no TUI chat final event

After:
TUI chat.send -> agent message tool source reply -> Gateway source-reply final event -> TUI sees reply
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.2 via repository wrappers
- Model/provider: live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` for the terminal TUI proof; mocked agent dispatch for focused regression tests
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway TUI/WebSocket client path
- Relevant config (redacted): default local dev config; secrets were not printed

### Steps

1. Applied the TUI source-reply regression test to `origin/main`; it failed with `missing TUI source reply; seen=[]`.
2. Ran the live negative control on exact base commit `5e0850fc548ceab82af7244938b833024e485834`; the TUI prompt submitted and the model used the message tool, but the active TUI did not render the standalone source reply.
3. Ran the same focused test after this patch; it passed and observed a TUI final event for the internal source reply.
4. Stopped the existing managed gateway, confirmed no listener remained on port `18789`, started a foreground PR-head gateway from this worktree at exact head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8`, and launched a real terminal TUI with `--session live-proof-20260520-0136`.
5. Manually submitted the same proof prompt in the real TUI PTY because `--message` did not visibly dispatch before connect.
6. Verified the TUI rendered `TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.` after the live agent used the `message` tool.
7. Queried `chat.history` for both base and PR-head runs to confirm the live tool calls, internal source sink results, delivery mirrors, model/provider, and completed run state.
8. Exited the TUI, stopped the foreground worktree gateway, and left the managed gateway inactive as allowed for this dev host.
9. Ran the focused dispatch/Gateway/media/refactor checks listed below.

### Expected

TUI receives a visible final chat event for the message-tool internal source reply, while durable transcript mirrors avoid raw `MEDIA:` paths and sensitive data URLs.

### Actual

The live base TUI did not render `OPENCLAW_TUI_BASE_NEGATIVE_20260519_1331` even though history showed the `message` tool sent that same text with `sourceReplySink=internal-ui` and persisted a delivery mirror. The live exact-head TUI rendered `TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.`; history showed the `message` tool sent that same text with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only` and `sourceReplySink=internal-ui`; and the regression tests passed for the Gateway/TUI source-reply event path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing live before + passing live after
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: live base failure where message-tool source reply was sent/persisted but not rendered in the active TUI, live terminal TUI source-reply delivery on exact pushed PR head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8`, live Codex harness provider invocation through `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, message-tool internal source sink delivery, TUI source-reply Gateway event delivery, source-reply media mirror upgrade, sensitive source media mirror suppression, metadata preservation through normalization/media/TTS, native non-UI route inheritance, and route non-inheritance for TUI/WebChat/Control UI source surfaces.
- Edge cases checked: image-only source replies, interactive/rich source replies, delayed source mirrors, dropped media, TTS synthesis, staged audio trust, and sensitive data-image replies.
- What you did **not** verify: live external messaging channel delivery such as Telegram/Discord/Slack.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The source-reply path now creates an additional Gateway final event for internal UI surfaces.
  - Mitigation: Uses distinct source-reply run ids and tests ensure normal external route behavior is unchanged.
- Risk: Internal surface classification could accidentally block route inheritance for native non-UI callers.
  - Mitigation: Native app ids are internal only in `mode=ui`; focused tests cover native `mode=node` inheriting the source route.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "delivers message-tool source replies to TUI clients over gateway chat events"`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat-reply-media.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/utils/message-channel.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-resolution.test.ts` (final refactor follow-up, 5 files / 225 tests passed in 17.87s)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-assistant-content.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-internal-source-reply.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile /tmp/openclaw-test-src-internal-chat-refactor-final2.tsbuildinfo --pretty false`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (clean after the final refactor: no actionable correctness issues found)
- Live exact-head proof on pushed head `7b838cc4307c6ab04b36d15253476ca472c253d8` with foreground worktree gateway + real terminal TUI session `live-proof-20260520-0136`; TUI rendered `TUI exact-head source reply 7b838cc 0136 visible.` and `chat.history` showed `sourceReplyDeliveryMode=message_tool_only` / `sourceReplySink=internal-ui`.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Contract Split

This PR is the first real doctor repair switchover after the structured health-check contract landed. The contract split is:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair?(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

`detect()` is the read-only source of truth for `doctor --lint` and post-repair validation. `repair()` is the mutating path used by `doctor --fix`. The migration goal is isomorphism: every converted repair should preserve the old user-facing behavior and contribution order while gaining a provable `detect -> repair -> detect` loop.

This slice converts the first real maintenance repairs:

- shell completion detection and cached-completion repair
- startup channel maintenance repair at the startup-channel contribution slot
- Linux systemd linger detection and guided repair for local Gateway services
- browser profile residue repair kept at the browser contribution slot from the prior repair slice

The important shape is that converted repairs no longer run from one early bulk `doctor:structured-health-repairs` pass. Each converted contribution invokes its mapped health check from its existing doctor position, so the contract changes without changing repair sequencing.

## Dry-Run, Diff, And Isomorphism

This PR also threads the future repair-preview contract through the converted paths:

- `dryRun` means the repair path can report what it would do without applying it.
- `diff: true` asks repair code to include structured config/file diffs when the repair has a renderable patch.
- `diff: false` does not skip the repair; it just means no structured diff payload is requested.
- side-effect repairs such as shell setup or systemd linger report `effects[]` instead of pretending to have a config/file diff.

The public doctor dry-run/diff mode should still be enabled at the end of the migration, not here. This branch prepares the contract and proves these first repairs behave isomorphically. Future repair branches should either return `diffs[]`, return side-effect `effects[]`, or explicitly document why preview is not meaningful for that repair.

## Migration Plan

This is an active staged migration, not a one-off refactor:

1. Land the contract and read-only checks. Done by the earlier doctor health-check PRs.
2. Convert real repairs in small buckets while preserving contribution order. This PR is the interactive-maintenance bucket.
3. For every converted repair, prove `detect -> repair -> detect` with focused tests and, where feasible, real CLI proof from disposable state.
4. Backfill dry-run/diff/effects metadata for each repair as it moves.
5. After the repair inventory is converted or classified, expose the user-facing doctor dry-run/diff mode with honest output for every repair category.

The remaining migration is currently planned as 6 buckets:

- legacy plugin manifests and configured plugin registry/install repairs
- sandbox registry, sandbox image, and sandbox scope repairs
- auth-profile repairs, including flat stores, OAuth sidecars, OAuth ids, keychain readiness, and Codex provider overrides
- session/state cleanup, including state integrity, Codex session routes, session locks, session transcripts, config-audit scrub, and legacy cron storage
- gateway/service repairs, including Gateway service config, Gateway daemon drift, memory recall/search repair, and prepared runtime-fact consumers
- final dry-run/diff enablement, once every repair bucket has either `diffs[]`, side-effect `effects[]`, or an explicit non-previewable classification with proof
## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Converted doctor maintenance repairs now run through structured health checks at their original doctor contribution positions, while preserving user-facing repair behavior and adding scoped post-repair validation.

Real environment tested: Current PR head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb` was tested in two ways: focused Windows source-checkout tests, plus a disposable Azure Ubuntu 24.04 VM with a real user systemd manager and real `loginctl` linger state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Azure proof ran current-head `doctor --lint` and `doctor --fix --yes` against disposable shell-completion and systemd-linger states. Focused proof ran:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node node_modules\oxfmt\bin\oxfmt --check --threads=1 src\commands\doctor-completion.test.ts src\commands\doctor.types.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.test.ts src\flows\doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src\flows\doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src\flows\doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false
```

Evidence after fix:

- Azure CLI transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-doctor-cli-proof.redacted.log
- Azure proof excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-proof-excerpt.txt
- Azure proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/summary.redacted.txt
- Focused test transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof.clean.log
- Focused proof excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof-excerpt.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/README.md
- Docker repro for the non-privileged shell-completion path: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/docker-repro.sh

Observed result after fix:

```text
shell completion lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
shell completion doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
shell completion lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd user manager status exit=0 expected=0
systemd disposable unit enabled exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger before fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
systemd linger doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
```

The focused proof also shows 7 focused files and 58 tests passing, changed-file formatting passing, and the core test TypeScript config exiting 0.

What was not tested: Docker was used only for the non-privileged shell-completion repro. The privileged systemd/loginctl behavior was intentionally proven on a disposable Azure VM instead of Docker because containerized loginctl proof is less representative than a real Linux user manager.

## Verification

- Focused Vitest proof: 7 files / 58 tests passed.
- Changed-file formatting: passed.
- Core test `tsgo`: passed.
- Disposable Azure CLI proof: shell-completion and systemd-linger both showed before-detect, repair, and after-detect success on current head.




## Stack Refresh: Repair Presentation Notes

This branch now also establishes the shared presentation-note contract for the rest of the doctor repair stack.

- `HealthRepairResult` can return ordered `notes[]` as `{ message, title }` presentation data.
- `runDoctorHealthRepairs()` preserves those notes alongside `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.
- The doctor contribution layer prints returned notes immediately after the structured repair call and before generic `Doctor changes` / `Doctor warnings` summaries, preserving contribution order without letting low-level helpers print directly.
- Note suppression still stays centralized in `note(...)`, so `OPENCLAW_SUPPRESS_NOTES` continues to suppress presentation output at the doctor layer.
- Existing repair callers forward notes from helper results where the helper contract already has them, so follow-on branches can remove direct `note(...)` calls from repair helpers without changing the runner again.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 2 focused files / 36 tests passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Detect/Repair Note Contract

This supersedes the earlier repair-only note wording in this PR body. The base contract now separates presentation by phase:

- `detect()` may return the legacy-compatible `HealthFinding[]` or the new `{ findings, notes }` object.
- Detect notes are read-only presentation context for normal `openclaw doctor`; they stay separate from lintable findings.
- `doctor --lint` normalizes detect output but keeps `notes` separate from `findings`, so lint exit codes and JSON-facing findings are not polluted by informational doctor notes.
- `runDoctorHealthRepairs()` now records `detectionNotes` separately from `repairNotes`.
- Repair implementations can return final `notes`, and can also use `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` for timing-sensitive repair presentation that must appear before/between repair steps without importing terminal UI.
- The doctor contribution layer owns printing, so `OPENCLAW_SUPPRESS_NOTES` remains centralized in `note(...)` and low-level helpers do not need terminal side effects.

Additional verification for this refinement:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 6 focused files / 55 tests passed, full repo `tsgo --noEmit` passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Converted Rules Use Note Phases

The converted checks in this PR now use the detect/repair presentation contract directly:

- Note-capturing readiness checks such as Claude CLI and browser readiness preserve warning/error-like output as findings, while informational note output is returned as detect notes instead of being dropped or printed from the helper.
- Workspace backup and memory-system suggestions are detect notes rather than lint findings, so normal `openclaw doctor` can render them without making `doctor --lint` noisy.
- The systemd linger repair emits its pre-confirmation/operator context through `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)`, preserving repair-time ordering while keeping terminal printing owned by the doctor layer.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 6 focused files / 55 tests passed, full repo `tsgo --noEmit` passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Repair-Note Sink Collector

The repair-note sink is now fully wired in the base #83753 contract. `runDoctorHealthRepairs()` injects a per-check `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` collector when invoking `repair()`, records those timing-sensitive notes in `repairNotes`, and forwards them to any outer sink. This makes the contract usable for upper-stack repairs such as sandbox and gateway helpers that need to present operator context before or between repair steps without calling `note(...)` directly.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot
```

Observed result: 3 focused files / 20 tests passed on head `2417269a00362a2de527962a00364100927137c2`.


## Stack Refresh: Shared Repair Presentation Prompter

This avoids per-helper sink parameters while keeping terminal rendering centralized: normal doctor prompters call `note(...)`, and structured health-check adapters can route the same method into `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` for ordered repair notes.

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-prompter.ts

```
