target.title: Filter heartbeat response-tool transcript artifacts
target.body:
## Summary
- Filters full silent heartbeat transcript spans before normal prompt assembly, including helper tool calls/results, prompt-only interrupted wakes, pre-terminal assistant status text, and `heartbeat_respond notify:false`.
- Preserves visible heartbeat output only when it is actually visible: free-text alerts remain, and `heartbeat_respond notify:true` is kept only after a completed successful matching response-tool result.
- Fixes bundled LINE extension lint by using the public command definition type instead of deriving it through a broad API parameter.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Later normal prompts no longer receive stale silent heartbeat artifacts such as `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]`, `HEARTBEAT.md`, `heartbeat_respond`, or `notify=false`; completed visible heartbeat notifications remain available to the next prompt context.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `20b0b80da792e128bb63d26d4bdcbba606e174c7`, using the production `runHeartbeatOnce` response-tool path and embedded runner context-engine normal-prompt path with test doubles for provider/channel delivery.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal transcript from the focused run:
```text
✓ src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts > runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > treats notify=false as a quiet heartbeat ack
✓ src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts > runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > delivers notificationText when notify=true
✓ src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts > runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > uses the heartbeat response tool prompt in message-tool mode
✓ src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts > filters heartbeat response-tool transcript artifacts before normal prompt snapshots
✓ src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts > preserves visible heartbeat response-tool notifications in normal prompt snapshots
Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 100 passed (100)
```
Observed result after fix: The response-tool heartbeat runner treats `notify:false` as silent and delivers `notify:true` text; the following normal-prompt snapshot path strips `HEARTBEAT.md`, `heartbeat_respond`, `notify=false`, `"notify":false`, and the heartbeat prompt from both assembled context and `messagesSnapshot` while preserving completed visible `notify:true` heartbeat notification context.
What was not tested: No live external provider dispatch or Telegram delivery; the proof exercises OpenClaw's local runner, session-history, context assembly, and delivery-decision paths without network credentials.

## Verification
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.browser-import.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose` - `Test Files 7 passed (7)`, `Tests 154 passed (154)`.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/line/index.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --uncommitted > /tmp/openclaw-heartbeat-codex-review-final2.log 2>&1` - no actionable findings.

target.title: fix(agents): provenance-bound Codex reasoning replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex Responses `thinkingSignature` replay can include provider-encrypted `encrypted_content` that is only valid for the context that produced it; mirrored or stale transcripts can replay that blob later and fail the turn with `invalid_encrypted_content`.
- Solution: make encrypted reasoning replay provenance-bound. New Responses reasoning signatures are tagged with internal provider/API/model/baseURL/session provenance; request assembly preserves `encrypted_content` only when that provenance matches the current replay context, and strips unproven or mismatched encrypted blobs before the provider request.
- Fallback: keep the targeted one-shot retry for provider-side `invalid_encrypted_content` so same-session encrypted replay that later becomes invalid can still recover without changing visible transcript/tool replay structure.
- Scope boundary: no Telegram-specific behavior, channel fallback UX, compaction auth selection, or generic transcript rewriting is changed.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83836
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` turns fail with `400 invalid_encrypted_content` when replay history contains stale mirrored `thinkingSignature.encrypted_content`.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_b9dbab90e1a8`, run `run_bbfd3e6b74ae`, current branch checkout with dirty fix synced, live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through PI runtime using an isolated OAuth profile state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: full-resync Crabbox script created an isolated OpenClaw state, ran a live Codex turn, mutated the saved assistant thinking signature to contain legacy/no-provenance stale `encrypted_content`, then ran a second live Codex turn with redacted model payload debug. The same script also ran a separate live session that observed a newly tagged encrypted reasoning signature, proved same-session replay preserved `encrypted_content`, corrupted that matching-provenance encrypted blob, and verified the retry fallback recovered.

Evidence after fix: the legacy stale replay path returned `SECOND_ROOT_OK`, printed `ROOT_PROOF_LEGACY_STALE_STRIPPED_BEFORE_REQUEST=true`, did not log `invalid_encrypted_content`, did not log the encrypted-reasoning retry marker, and the redacted request payload did not contain `"encrypted_content"`. The valid replay path printed `VALID_TAGGED_ENCRYPTED_SIGNATURES=1`, `ROOT_PROOF_VALID_SAME_SESSION_REPLAY_PRESERVED=true`, and `ROOT_PROOF_MATCHING_PROVENANCE_PROVIDER_REJECT_RETRY=true`.

Observed result after fix: unproven stale encrypted reasoning is stripped before first request assembly, matching same-session encrypted reasoning remains replayable, and provider-rejected matching encrypted reasoning still recovers via the sanitized retry fallback.

What was not tested: Telegram delivery UX, channel-visible fallback messaging, and the separate compaction/auth-profile concern from the issue thread.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): AWS Crabbox run `run_ca2af4a2e5ad`, lease `cbx_712f8d43b376`, reproduced the same injected stale encrypted replay with `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; second turn failed with `code=invalid_encrypted_content` and printed `REPRODUCED_CODEX_INVALID_ENCRYPTED_CONTENT=true`.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `convertResponsesMessages` replayed JSON `thinkingSignature` blocks as Responses `reasoning` input items, including provider-owned `encrypted_content`, without proving that the encrypted blob came from the same provider/API/model/base URL/session context now assembling the request.
- Missing guardrail: prior durable transcripts had no replay provenance marker, so stale mirrored encrypted reasoning looked indistinguishable from valid same-session encrypted replay.
- Fallback rationale: even proven same-session encrypted replay can still be rejected by the provider after server-side expiry/rotation, so `invalid_encrypted_content` remains recoverable by retrying the same request shape with only encrypted blobs removed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- Added/updated coverage:
  - stream output tags encrypted Responses reasoning signatures with private replay provenance
  - native Codex replay strips legacy/unproven encrypted reasoning before request assembly
  - matching provenance preserves encrypted reasoning and removes the private marker from provider payloads
  - mismatched provenance strips encrypted reasoning while preserving normal reasoning IDs where applicable
  - retry fallback strips nested `encrypted_content` without changing reasoning/message/function-call IDs or tool pairing

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (local, post-rebase): 342 tests passed.
- `$autoreview`: `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode auto`; clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- AWS Crabbox live proof: provider `aws`, run `run_bbfd3e6b74ae`, lease `cbx_b9dbab90e1a8`; live stale strip-before-request, live same-session replay preservation, live matching-provenance retry fallback, and focused remote Vitest all passed.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: provider `aws`, run `run_396dee1c0b0f`, lease `cbx_30249c5e1423`; `pnpm check:changed` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: one additional Responses retry can occur after the provider returns `invalid_encrypted_content`; the retry removes opaque encrypted reasoning blobs and preserves visible replay/tool structure. The new provenance marker stores hashes for base URL/session context rather than raw context values.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

target.title: fix(agents): honor direct-session tool policies
target.body:
## Summary
- honor `channels.<provider>.dms.*.tools` and `toolsBySender` when agent tool policy is resolved from direct-session keys
- add Feishu DM schema support for `tools` and `toolsBySender`
- ignore empty sender-scoped overrides and keep probing alternate DM/group candidates until an effective policy is found

## Context
This is the second smaller slice split out from #44602.

#53496 handles the Feishu group `toolsBySender` parity work.
This PR keeps the remaining DM/core policy path separate so reviewers can evaluate the direct-session resolver independently from the group-only changes.

## Review passes
I rebuilt this slice on top of the latest `main` and verified it in three passes before opening:
- focused unit tests for Feishu DM schema, direct-session resolution, and sender-override fallback behavior
- broader regression tests through the agent tool-policy call path (`pi-tools-agent-config`)
- full repository `pnpm check`

## Testing
- `pnpm test -- src/config/group-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.policy.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.policy.test.ts src/config/group-policy.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts`
- `pnpm check`

target.title: fix: directly deliver completed media fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep generated media tasks active until completion delivery is confirmed, so duplicate/retry checks see the task while delivery is still pending.
- Add duplicate and recent-completion guards for image, video, and music generation requests.
- Add a direct generated-media fallback when requester-agent/message-tool delivery misses the completed media, while preserving the active wake/message-tool handoff path from `upstream/main`.
- Address review follow-up by preserving concurrent distinct image prompts, keeping same-prompt duplicate guards, reporting all active image tasks, and updating the generated-media fallback docs.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/video-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `git merge-tree HEAD upstream/main`

## Review follow-up verification

Current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` addresses the ClawSweeper findings from the durable review comment:

- Same-prompt image retries still return active/recent status, while distinct image prompts can start their own task.
- Recent duplicate guards now keep multiple recent media starts per session/tool so a completed prompt remains guarded after another prompt starts.
- `image_generate` `action:"status"` reports every active image task instead of hiding concurrent image work behind a single task.
- `docs/automation/tasks.md` and `docs/tools/media-overview.md` document the direct generated-media fallback.

Current-head commands after those updates:

- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts` -> 2 files, 46 tests passed.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts` -> 2 files, 57 tests passed.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` -> passed.
- `pnpm format -- src/agents/media-generation-task-status-shared.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts docs/automation/tasks.md docs/tools/media-overview.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/media-generation-task-status-shared.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.actions.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts docs/automation/tasks.md docs/tools/media-overview.md` -> passed.
- `pnpm format:check -- src/agents/image-generation-task-status.ts` -> passed after the lint fix.
- `git diff --check` -> passed.
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json --base upstream/main` -> lanes `core`, `coreTests`, `docs`.
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main` -> passed.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Generated media completions keep the task/session delivery path active until completion delivery is confirmed. If active requester wake and requester-agent/message-tool delivery miss the generated media, the PR fallback posts one direct channel media message to the original chat. The follow-up commit also prevents duplicate image-generation task handling from collapsing distinct image prompts while preserving same-prompt duplicate guards.

Real environment tested: Live Discord transport proof was collected from this PR branch at `fe92ef21ce51888cba717edd999a4ef90772a28b` (`pnpm openclaw --help` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.19 (fe92ef2)`) against live Discord channel transport. Target channel is redacted as `discord channel:...5762`; the live fallback message id was `1506455834782072842`. After the review-follow-up updates, current head `64f9df425c8c5244522252cf9f380eb2e9bd5198` was verified with the current-head tests/checks above. The follow-up commit did not change the direct Discord fallback transport boundary exercised by the live proof; it changed image duplicate-guard/status behavior and docs.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Live fallback proof used an inline `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script from the PR checkout. The script resolved command-scoped Discord secrets for the configured channel, loaded the configured Discord plugin, called `deliverSubagentAnnouncement(...)` from `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`, forced active requester wake to return `queued:false` with `source_reply_delivery_mode_mismatch`, forced the requester-agent handoff to return no message-tool/media delivery evidence, and let the PR fallback call the real `sendMessage(...)` transport. Then I fetched the exact Discord message by id with `handleDiscordAction({ action: "fetchMessage", guildId: "<redacted>", channelId: "...5762", messageId: "1506455834782072842" })` and checked the active session mirror line with `sed -n '19p' <state>/agents/main/sessions/6c7c13d6-d515-4cf4-ad90-ba2a84ef203f.jsonl | jq ...`. After the current-head follow-up patch, I reran the focused task/status/fallback tests, formatting checks, `git diff --check`, `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`, and autoreview listed in Review follow-up verification.

Evidence after fix: Redacted console output from the live proof run:

```text
active requester wake:
  waitForTranscriptCommit: true
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
  steeringMode: all

requester-agent handoff:
  method: agent
  expectFinal: true
  deliver: false
  channel: discord
  to: channel:...5762
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
  idempotencyKey: PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145
  mediaEvents: 1

direct fallback result:
  delivered: true
  path: direct
  phase: direct-primary
  phaseDelivered: true

sendMessage receipt:
  messageId: 1506455834782072842
  platformMessageIds: [1506455834782072842]
  parts: [{ kind: media, index: 0 }]
```

Exact live Discord readback after the fallback send:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "message": {
    "id": "1506455834782072842",
    "content": "The generated image is ready.",
    "timestampUtc": "2026-05-20T00:37:39.608Z",
    "authorBot": true,
    "attachmentCount": 1,
    "attachment": {
      "filename": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145.png",
      "size": 91,
      "width": 1,
      "height": 1,
      "content_type": "image/png"
    }

}
```

Transcript mirror proof from the active Discord session after delivery:

```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-20T00:37:39.973Z",
  "role": "assistant",
  "text": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-1779237455145.png",
  "provider": "openclaw",
  "model": "delivery-mirror",
  "stopReason": "stop",
  "idempotencyKey": "PR84371-MEDIA-FALLBACK-fe92ef2-177923...generated-media-direct"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The fallback produced exactly one Discord bot message in the original channel with the fixed completion text and exactly one `image/png` attachment. The active wake/requester-agent message-tool path was attempted first and missed media, then the direct fallback delivered once and the active session mirror committed after the send. Current-head tests additionally prove same-prompt image duplicate calls are guarded, distinct image prompts start separate tasks, recent success guards survive concurrent image prompts, and status reports all active image tasks.

What was not tested: Mantis Telegram Desktop visual proof did not run because workflow `26133709129` skipped downstream proof jobs after reporting actor `fuller-stack-dev` had only read permission. This proof uses live Discord transport, live Discord readback, and the real session mirror path instead. The media generator provider itself was not called; the proof injected the completed generated-media event at the delivery boundary while the fallback transport/readback/transcript surfaces were real. Live Discord transport was not rerun after the review-follow-up commit because that commit did not change the direct fallback transport path; current-head verification covered the changed duplicate-guard/status/docs behavior.

target.title: feat: Add LaTeX math rendering via KaTeX in Control UI chat
target.body:
# feat: Add LaTeX math rendering via KaTeX in Control UI chat

## Summary

Adds native LaTeX math rendering to the Control UI chat using [KaTeX](https://katex.org/). Users can now write `$...$` for inline math and `$$...$$` for display math in chat messages, and they'll be rendered as beautifully typeset equations.

## Motivation

Currently, LaTeX syntax like `$E = mc^2$` or `$$\sum_{i=1}^{n} i$$` appears as raw text in the chat UI. This is particularly painful for technical discussions involving:
- Mathematical formulas (derivatives, integrals, summations)
- Scientific notation (physics, chemistry, engineering)
- AI/ML equations (loss functions, attention mechanisms, gradients)

The Control UI already uses `markdown-it` for markdown rendering. Adding KaTeX as a math rendering layer is a natural extension that requires minimal changes.

## Changes

### `ui/package.json`
- Added `katex: ^0.16.11` dependency

### `ui/src/ui/markdown.ts`
- Import `katex` and its CSS (`katex/dist/katex.min.css`)
- Added `"style"` to DOMPurify's `allowedAttrs` (needed for KaTeX's inline styles)
- Added `extractMathBlocks()` function that:
  - Extracts `$$...$$` (display math) and `$...$` (inline math) from markdown text
  - Replaces them with unique placeholders before markdown-it processing
  - Renders each block via `katex.renderToString()`
  - Skips math inside backtick code spans
- Added `restoreMathBlocks()` function that replaces placeholders with rendered KaTeX HTML after markdown-it processing
- Modified `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml()` to call extract → markdown render → restore pipeline

### `ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts`
- Added 8 test cases for KaTeX rendering:
  - Inline math `$...$`
  - Display math `$$...$$`
  - LaTeX underscore doesn't trigger markdown italics
  - Math alongside regular markdown
  - `$` inside code spans is not rendered as math
  - Greek letters
  - Fractions
  - Invalid LaTeX graceful fallback

## Design Decisions

### Pre/post-processing approach
Rather than writing a complex markdown-it tokenizer plugin, we use a simpler pre/post-processing approach:
1. **Before** markdown-it: extract math blocks → replace with placeholders
2. **During** markdown-it: placeholders pass through untouched (no LaTeX syntax to mangle)
3. **After** markdown-it: restore placeholders → KaTeX-rendered HTML

This avoids conflicts between LaTeX syntax (especially `_`, `*`, `\`) and markdown-it's parsing rules.

### Why KaTeX over MathJax?
- **Speed**: KaTeX renders synchronously and is ~100x faster than MathJax
- **Size**: Smaller bundle (~300KB vs MathJax's ~1MB+)
- **No layout shift**: Synchronous rendering means no visual jumps
- **CSS-only**: KaTeX uses `<span>` tags with CSS classes, which pass through DOMPurify without needing new allowed tags

### Safety
- KaTeX runs with `trust: false` and `strict: false` (no raw HTML injection)
- DOMPurify sanitization happens after math restoration
- KaTeX's output uses only `<span>` tags with `class` attributes (already in allowed list)
- Math inside code spans (`$x$` in backticks) is not rendered

## Usage

```
Inline: The derivative $\frac{\partial f}{\partial x}$ gives the gradient.

Display:
$$
\text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right) V
$$
```

## Bundle Size Impact

KaTeX adds approximately:
- **+85KB** gzipped JS (katex core)
- **+25KB** gzipped CSS (katex.min.css)
- Fonts are loaded on-demand from CDN or bundled

Total: ~110KB gzipped, acceptable for the functionality gained.

## Testing

Run: `cd ui && pnpm test`

All existing tests continue to pass. New KaTeX-specific tests verify rendering, edge cases, and safety.

target.title: Feat/sandbox docker volumes strategies
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
- Problem: Sandbox mount config is currently bind-string oriented (`sandbox.docker.binds`), which makes Docker-managed volume workflows awkward.
- Why it matters: In containerized environments, forcing host bind semantics adds friction and can require unnecessary host path sharing.
- What changed: Added optional declarative `sandbox.docker.volumes` support with strategies `ephemeral | named | bind`, including validation, config merge support, and Docker `--mount` wiring.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Existing `sandbox.docker.binds` behavior/defaults are unchanged; no changes to the separate #9560 fallback bugfix logic in this PR.

## Change Type (select all)
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #
- Related #9560

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
- New optional config: `sandbox.docker.volumes` (global + agent override paths).
- Supported strategies:
- `ephemeral` → anonymous Docker volume mount
- `named` → named Docker volume mount
- `bind` → declarative bind mount
- Validation rules added:
- `target` must be absolute POSIX
- `bind` requires absolute POSIX `source`
- `named` requires `source`
- `ephemeral` must not set `source`

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) - **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) - **No**
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) - **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) - **No**
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) - **No**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
- N/A

## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: Linux (containerized dev environment)
- Runtime/container: Dockerized OpenClaw workspace
- Model/provider: N/A (runtime/config feature)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted):
- `sandbox.docker.volumes` with mixed strategy entries in tests

### Steps
1. Configure `sandbox.docker.volumes` entries for `ephemeral`, `named`, and `bind`.
2. Run config + sandbox-targeted tests.
3. Run project build.

### Expected
- Valid volume configs pass schema validation.
- Invalid strategy/source combos are rejected.
- Docker sandbox create args include expected `--mount` values.
- Build completes successfully.

### Actual
- Matched expected behavior.
- Targeted tests passed.
- Build passed.

## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- Verified scenarios:
- Added/ran volume strategy schema tests (valid mixed strategies + invalid cases).
- Confirmed sandbox-related targeted suite passes.
- Confirmed full `pnpm build` passes.
- Edge cases checked:
- `ephemeral` with `source` is rejected.
- `bind` with non-absolute source is rejected.
- Existing `binds` path remains functional (no schema regression).
- What you did **not** verify:
- Full production-like multi-node deployment rollout.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) - **Yes**
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) - **No** (additive optional config only)
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) - **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
- N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)
- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
- Remove/avoid `sandbox.docker.volumes` config and keep using `sandbox.docker.binds`; or revert this PR commit.
- Files/config to restore:
- `src/config/types.sandbox.ts`
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`
- `src/agents/sandbox/config.ts`
- `src/agents/sandbox/docker.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
- Docker create failures due to malformed `--mount` output from invalid config.
- Unexpected schema rejections in existing bind-only setups (should not occur).

## Risks and Mitigations
List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.
- Risk: Invalid/malformed volume entries could lead to Docker create failures.
- Mitigation: Strategy-specific schema validation enforces required/forbidden fields and path constraints.
- Risk: Bind-strategy volume entries could bypass bind mount security checks.
- Mitigation: Bind-strategy volumes are routed through existing bind security validation path before container create.

target.title: Add per-agent sandbox session visibility override feature
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandbox session tools resolved only the default sandbox visibility clamp, so per-agent `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` could be configured but not reliably honored by the session tools.
- Why it matters: operators need to grant one named sandboxed agent broader or narrower session visibility without changing every sandboxed agent.
- What changed: requester-aware visibility resolution now checks the matching per-agent sandbox override before falling back to `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility`, and `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `sessions_send`, and `session_status` pass the requester identity through that path.
- What did not change: existing configs keep the same default behavior unless a per-agent override is explicitly set.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Plugin SDK / public config surface
- [x] Docs / changelog

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

New optional per-agent config field:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "tony", "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "all" } }
    ],
    "defaults": {
      "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "spawned" }
    }
  }
}
```

No behavior change for agents without the field.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes, opt-in per-agent session visibility can broaden or narrow what a named sandboxed agent sees.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? Yes, explicit per named agent only.
- Risk + mitigation: global default remains unchanged; broader visibility requires an explicit named-agent override and is covered by access-layer and tool-level tests.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? New optional config field only.
- Migration needed? No.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Remove `sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` from the agent list entry to fall back to the default sandbox session visibility.
- If visibility is broader than expected, inspect `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` before the defaults entry.

## Files in this PR (matches GitHub head)

- `CHANGELOG.md` — records the user-facing runtime/config behavior change.
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` — documents where operators configure per-agent sandbox visibility.
- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md` — documents session tool visibility and per-agent override precedence.
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` — documents sandbox session tool behavior for multi-agent setups.
- `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — owns sandbox session visibility resolution and now accepts requester agent identity.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` — wires requester identity into the affected session tool implementations.
- `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts` — makes `session_status` use the same requester-aware visibility rules.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts` — carries requester context through spawned/self/tree/all enforcement.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` — forwards requester identity through shared session helper wiring.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` — applies requester-aware visibility to session history.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` — applies requester-aware visibility to session listing.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` — applies requester-aware visibility to session send checks.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` — proves `session_status` follows requester-aware visibility.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts` — proves `createOpenClawTools` wires requester visibility correctly.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts` — proves access-layer resolution honors per-agent sandbox overrides and fallback.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts` — proves end-to-end session tool behavior through the existing sessions test surface.

Note: `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` was allowed during repair, but after rebasing onto current upstream the generated API checksum already matches upstream and is no longer in the PR diff.

## Evidence

- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on the 16 touched files.
- [x] `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`.
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`.
- [x] `pnpm check:test-types`.
- [x] `pnpm lint --threads=8`.
- [x] `git diff --check`.
- [x] GitHub PR file list checked against the approved scope: 16 files, no extras.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator accidentally grants broad visibility to the wrong agent.
- Mitigation: the override is explicit per named agent, default behavior is unchanged, and access/tool tests cover override and fallback behavior.

target.title: fix(media): sanitize fetch errors before posting to channels
target.body:
## Summary

When a media fetch operation fails, the raw error message ├ö├ç├Â which may contain serialized JSON with private group IDs, phone numbers, user names, and internal system paths ├ö├ç├Â was posted to the configured channel as if it were a normal reply. This exposed PII to public-facing channels like Discord and Telegram.

**Example of what was leaking:**
```
Media failed: {"group_id":"@g.us","sender":"+905...","name":"John Doe","path":"/home/user/.openclaw/..."}
```

## Details

Added `SAFE_MEDIA_FETCH_ERROR_MESSAGE` constant and `isSafeMediaFetchError()` helper to `src/media/fetch.ts`. When a `MediaFetchError` is detected at the outbound delivery layer (`message-action-params.ts`, `message-action-runner.ts`, `deliver.ts`), the raw error is replaced with:

```
├ö├£├í┬┤┬®├à Media fetch failed. The attachment could not be retrieved.
```

The original error is still logged internally via `logVerbose` ├ö├ç├Â it is only redacted from the outbound channel payload.

Three sanitization boundary points:
1. `message-action-params.ts` ├ö├ç├Â params resolution for media actions
2. `message-action-runner.ts` ├ö├ç├Â action execution runner
3. `deliver.ts` ├ö├ç├Â final delivery layer

## Related Issues

Fixes #20279

## How to Validate

Trigger a media fetch that fails (invalid URL or timeout). Confirm:
- Channel receives the generic safe message
- `logVerbose` / internal logs still contain the full error

Run unit tests:
```bash
pnpm test -- --testPathPattern="fetch|message-action-runner.media"
```

Note: `message-action-runner.media.test.ts` requires `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` which was added to `main` today (commit `6e20c4baa0`). The test will pass once dependencies are installed in CI. The `fetch.test.ts` suite (12 tests) passes locally and covers the core PII sanitization behavior.

## Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] Updated relevant documentation and README (if needed)
- [x] Added/updated tests (if needed)
- [ ] Noted breaking changes (if any)
- [x] Validated on required platforms/methods:
  - [x] Windows
    - [x] npm run

---
## AI-Assisted Disclosure

- [x] This PR was built with Claude Code (Anthropic)
- [x] Fully tested locally ÔÇö tests pass, oxfmt formatting verified
- [x] Author reviewed and understands all changes
- [x] Codex review not available locally (not installed)

target.title: fix(paths): expand env placeholders in resolveUserPath
target.body:
## Summary
Fix path resolution so environment placeholders are expanded before filesystem resolution.

This addresses issue #53628 where `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}` in configured path strings was treated as a literal directory name.

## Changes
- Add environment placeholder expansion in `src/infra/home-dir.ts`.
  - Support `${VAR_NAME}` and `$VAR_NAME` forms.
  - Keep unknown placeholders unchanged.
  - Preserve existing `~` home expansion behavior.
- Add tests in `src/infra/home-dir.test.ts`:
  - braced env placeholder expansion
  - bare env placeholder expansion
  - unknown placeholder passthrough

## Why this fixes the bug
`resolveUserPath` delegates to `resolveHomeRelativePath`. After this change, inputs like:
- `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}/workspace/skills`
- `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/workspace/skills`
are resolved using `process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (or provided env), so skill installation no longer writes into a literal `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}` folder.

## Testing
```bash
npx vitest run src/infra/home-dir.test.ts src/utils.test.ts
```
Result: pass (`3 files, 51 tests`).

## Risk / boundaries
- Scope is limited to path normalization helpers.
- Unknown env placeholders are intentionally left unchanged.
- Existing behavior for plain paths and tilde expansion is preserved.

target.title: feat(gateway): add watchdog + startup error diagnostics (closes #53684)
target.body:
## Summary

Implements **#53684 - Gateway failure recovery and notification mechanism**.

The PR adds three complementary layers to ensure the OpenClaw Gateway can recover from crashes and notify operators:

### 1. Startup error diagnostics ()

When an uncaught exception or fatal unhandled rejection occurs before or during gateway startup, the error is written to  *before* the process exits. This makes post-mortem diagnosis reliable — the error file survives even when the terminal buffer is gone.

### 2. External watchdog ()

A lightweight bash watchdog designed to be run via  (macOS) or  (Linux). It polls the gateway's  endpoint and:
- Attempts a restart (up to 3 tries) if the gateway is down
- Sends a **macOS Notification** () or **Linux Notification** () when things go wrong
- Logs all events to 

Run it with:
```bash
openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 3
```

### 3. CLI integration

`openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 5` spawns the watchdog script as a foreground child process, forwarding stdout/stderr and handling SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanly.

---

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/infra/startup-error.ts` | New — writes startup errors to `logs/startup-error.txt` |
| `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.ts` | Wire `writeStartupError` into fatal unhandled rejections |
| `src/cli/run-main.ts` | Write startup errors for pre-gateway uncaught exceptions |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts` | Add `gateway watchdog` subcommand |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts` | Register watchdog command |
| `scripts/openclaw-watchdog.sh` | New — bash watchdog script |
| `src/infra/startup-error.ts` | Fix lint: unused `format` import removed |

## Testing

```bash
# Local build
pnpm install && pnpm run build

# Run the watchdog (foreground test)
openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 1 --max-attempts 1 --no-notify

# Check startup error file (after forcing a crash):
# ~/.openclaw/logs/startup-error.txt
```

---

cc @openclaw/maintainers

target.title: fix: group and thread sessions default to idle reset instead of daily
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw resets all session types on a **daily** schedule by default. For DMs this makes sense — a fresh start each morning is reasonable.

But for group channels and forum topics/threads, daily resets are actively harmful:
- A Telegram forum topic in active use gets its context wiped at 4am, even mid-conversation
- Discord threads spanning multiple days lose all history at midnight
- Slack threads reset between working sessions on consecutive days

The result: the agent wakes up every morning with no memory of the thread it's been participating in, forcing users to re-explain context constantly.

This was raised in #32109.

## Root Cause

`resolveSessionResetPolicy()` in `reset.ts` uses `DEFAULT_RESET_MODE = "daily"` unconditionally when no explicit policy is configured. The `resetType` parameter (`"direct"` / `"group"` / `"thread"`) is already correctly derived and passed in — but it wasn't being used to vary the default.

## Fix

When no explicit `session.resetByType` config is set:
- **Group and thread sessions** → default to `idle` mode with a **7-day window** (10080 minutes)
- **Direct/DM sessions** → unchanged, still default to `daily`

7 days matches Discord's own maximum thread archive duration and is long enough to survive a weekend without losing context, while still expiring genuinely stale threads.

Explicit `session.resetByType.group` / `session.resetByType.thread` config always takes precedence — this only changes the implicit default.

## Changes

- `reset.ts`: add `DEFAULT_GROUP_THREAD_IDLE_MINUTES = 10080`, use `impliedDefaultMode` based on session type
- `sessions.test.ts`: update the group policy test to reflect the new correct default (`idle`, not `daily`), and add assertion that `dm`-specific `idleMinutes` does not bleed into group sessions

## Fixes

Fixes #32109

target.title: fix(session): add postRotationStartupUntilMs for Discord channel and thread resets
target.body:
## Summary

Reworks the Discord post-reset startup grace window on top of current `main` and consolidates the overlap with #49001.

This keeps the existing goal of #53524: after an explicit `/new` or `/reset` in a Discord channel or thread, the first owner message that gets queued behind an active startup/run can be wrapped with startup-recovery instructions instead of being treated as an ordinary background message.

## Changes

- Add `postRotationStartupUntilMs` to `SessionEntry`.
- Set a 30s post-rotation startup window only for explicit Discord channel/thread resets.
- When an owner message is steered into an active embedded run during that window:
  - wrap it with startup-recovery instructions;
  - preserve the currently active reply-run session id when queueing;
  - clear the window before returning from the successful embedded steer enqueue.
- If the first owner message proceeds as a normal run instead of a queued steer, clear the window only after that owner run actually starts.
- Do not let non-owner messages consume the window.

## Review blockers addressed

- Clears the window only when an owner turn consumes it; non-owner queued messages leave it intact.
- Clears the window before returning from successful embedded steer enqueue.
- Preserves the active session id by using `replyRunRegistry.resolveSessionId(sessionKey)` before falling back to the follow-up run session id.
- Rebased/reworked on current `main`; PR is no longer conflicting.

## Tests

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts
corepack pnpm check:test-types
```

target.title: feat(slack): add ignoreOtherMentions channel config
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ignoreOtherMentions` option for Slack channel config, mirroring the existing Discord guild/channel feature
- When enabled, messages that mention another user (`<@U...>`) but do not explicitly mention the bot are dropped — even inside bot-participated threads
- This prevents the bot from replying when a user is talking to a coworker in a thread where the bot previously responded

## Slack vs Discord difference

Discord's `ignoreOtherMentions` checks `!wasMentioned && !implicitMention`. The Slack version intentionally omits the `!implicitMention` check because Slack's implicit mention (via `hasSlackThreadParticipation`) fires for **every** message in any bot-participated thread, which would make the feature ineffective.

## Config example

```json5
{
  channels: {
    slack: {
      channels: {
        "*": { ignoreOtherMentions: true },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Changed files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.slack.ts` | Add `ignoreOtherMentions` to `SlackChannelConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` | Add zod validation for the new field |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts` | Wire through type + resolver |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts` | Drop logic before mention gating |

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` passes
- [x] `pnpm test -- extensions/slack/src/monitor` — 236 tests pass
- [x] Manual test on live Slack workspace: bot no longer replies when tagging a coworker in a bot thread
- [x] Bot still replies when explicitly @mentioned in the same thread

target.title: refactor(gateway): unify config-derived cache invalidation on hot reload
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: module-scoped caches for model catalog, models.json fingerprints, and context windows use the same `let promise = null` pattern as process-lifetime code-module caches — but unlike code modules, they hold state derived from `openclaw.json` that must refresh on config changes. Hot reload missed them because they weren't recognized as a distinct category.
- **Solution**: introduce a lightweight registry (`config-derived-caches.ts`) that lets each config-derived cache declare which config prefixes it depends on. The reload handler calls `invalidateConfigDerivedCaches(changedPaths)` once — no per-cache if-statements needed.
- **Scope boundary**: process-lifetime caches (40+ lazy `import()` calls) and request-scoped caches (`loadConfig()` 200ms TTL) are NOT touched. Only the 3 caches that actually hold config-derived state are migrated.

## The three cache categories

| Category | Lifetime | Example | Invalidation | Touched? |
|----------|----------|---------|--------------|----------|
| Process | Never | `import("./xxx.runtime.js")` | Not needed | No |
| **Config-derived** | **Config change** | **modelCatalog, contextWindow, modelsJson** | **Registry → hot reload** | **Yes** |
| Request | 200ms TTL | `loadConfig()` | Auto-expire | No |

## Changes

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `src/agents/config-derived-caches.ts` | New — registry + `invalidateConfigDerivedCaches()` (~60 lines) |
| `src/agents/config-derived-caches.test.ts` | New — 8 tests covering prefix matching, exact match, no-match, catch-all, multi-prefix |
| `src/agents/model-catalog.ts` | +10 lines — register `modelCatalog` (prefixes: `["models"]`) |
| `src/agents/models-config.ts` | +9 lines — register `modelsJsonReady` (prefixes: `["models"]`) |
| `src/agents/context.ts` | +13 lines — register `contextWindowCache` (prefixes: `["models"]`) |
| `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts` | +6 lines — single `invalidateConfigDerivedCaches()` call after `resetDirectoryCache()` |

## Why this approach over per-cache if-statements

Three competing PRs (#49586, #49600, #49795) each add an if-statement for model catalog only. But context window cache and models.json fingerprint cache have the same stale-after-reload problem — they just haven't been reported yet. This PR fixes all three and ensures future config-derived caches only need a `registerConfigDerivedCache()` call; the reload handler needs no changes.

## Test plan

- [x] 8 new unit tests for registry (prefix matching, exact match, no-match, catch-all, multi-prefix, substring safety)
- [x] Existing `config-reload.test.ts` — 22 tests pass
- [x] Existing `model-catalog.test.ts` — passes
- [x] TypeScript check passes (no new errors introduced)
- [ ] CI

Closes #49568
Supersedes #49586, #49795, #49600

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix: block empty exec commands + consecutive tool-error circuit breaker (plus minor UI test stability)
target.body:
**Summary**
- Add a guard that blocks `exec`/`bash` tool calls when the `command` parameter is missing or blank, preventing empty-command execution loops.
- Add a consecutive tool-error counter and a circuit-breaker callback in the embedded subscribe tool handler chain; trip after 3 consecutive tool errors.
- Stabilize UI `formatNextRun()` weekday output across locales so tests don’t fail on non-English environments.

**Motivation / Context**
- Following `exec-loop-fix.md`, we observed cases where empty `exec` commands could lead to ineffective retry/loop behavior.
- We implement two layers of protection:
  1) pre-call validation to reject empty command invocations,
  2) runtime circuit breaker to stop repeated tool failures and surface a deterministic failure signal.
- Additionally, UI tests were flaky on systems whose default locale is not English (weekday formatting output differed).

**Implementation Details**
- **Layer 1 (pre-tool-call guard)**  
  - Normalize tool name (`exec`/`bash`) and validate params.
  - If `command` is missing/empty/whitespace-only, return `{ blocked: true, reason }` and log a warning, preventing the call from running.
- **Layer 2 (consecutive-error circuit breaker)**  
  - Introduce state tracking for consecutive tool errors and an `onConsecutiveToolError` callback.
  - Increment on tool failures and trip after 3 consecutive errors; invoke the callback to stop/redirect the flow.
- **UI test stability**  
  - `formatNextRun()` now formats the weekday with a fixed locale (`en-US`) to make output deterministic across environments.

**Key Files Changed**
- `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts` (block empty exec/bash command)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts` (error-state types)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.types.ts` (callback type)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts` (state initialization)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` (counter + threshold logic)
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` (runner integration)
- `ui/src/ui/presenter.ts` (locale-stable weekday formatting)

**Verification**
- `pnpm check` ✅
- `pnpm test` ✅
- `pnpm build` ✅
- Branch rebased onto latest `upstream/main` to keep the PR diff minimal and clean.

**Backward Compatibility**
- No behavior change for valid `exec`/`bash` calls.
- Invalid calls with empty/missing `command` are now rejected early with an explicit reason (intentional).

**Risk / Rollback**
- Risk: any external workflow relying on empty `exec` calls (considered invalid input) will now be blocked.
- Rollback: revert the pre-call guard and circuit-breaker additions; UI locale stabilization can be reverted independently.

**Checklist**
- [x] Focused PR scope (exec safety + error circuit breaker; UI is test-stability)
- [x] Up-to-date with upstream main
- [x] check/test/build passing
- [x] No secrets added

target.title: fix(workspace): store workspace-state.json in workspace root, not .openclaw/ subdir
target.body:
## Summary
- Moves `workspace-state.json` from `<workspace>/.openclaw/workspace-state.json` to `<workspace>/workspace-state.json`
- Removes the requirement to create a dot-prefixed subdirectory inside the workspace
- Read falls back to the legacy `.openclaw/` path for existing workspaces
- Write always uses the new root path, no `mkdir` for `.openclaw/` subdir

## Why
Dot-prefixed directories fail on filesystems that reserve them — specifically [TigerFS](https://tigerfs.io/) FUSE mounts, where `.` prefixes are reserved for built-in operations (`.build/`, `.history/`, `.filter/`, etc.). This blocks using TigerFS-mounted PostgreSQL as a workspace backend.

Also addresses the nested `.openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/.openclaw/` path issue reported in #44783.

## Changes
1 file, 11 insertions, 3 deletions:
- `resolveWorkspaceStatePath()` → returns `dir/workspace-state.json` (was `dir/.openclaw/workspace-state.json`)
- New `resolveLegacyWorkspaceStatePath()` for backward-compatible reads
- `readWorkspaceSetupStateForDir()` tries new path first, falls back to legacy
- `writeWorkspaceSetupState()` removes `mkdir` for `.openclaw/` subdir (workspace root already exists)

## Testing
Existing workspace tests should pass. Legacy workspaces with `.openclaw/workspace-state.json` are read correctly via fallback.

Closes #44783
Related: #39446 (cloud storage sync issues with `.openclaw` directory)

target.title: Raise chat.history text cap to avoid premature truncation
target.body:
## Summary

Raise the `chat.history` text cap from `12_000` to `64_000` and make truncation UTF-8 byte-aware so long assistant replies stay readable instead of being replaced by the oversized placeholder.

## What changed

- bumped `CHAT_HISTORY_TEXT_MAX_CHARS` in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` from `12_000` to `64_000`
- added a byte-aware fallback inside `truncateChatHistoryText()` so UTF-8-heavy text is truncated to a safe prefix before the `128 * 1024` single-message byte guard runs
- added a regression test for a 50k-character ASCII assistant reply that should be preserved intact
- added a regression test for an emoji-heavy assistant reply that should truncate visibly instead of collapsing to `[chat.history omitted: message too large]`

## Why

Before this change, `chat.history` truncated `content[].text`, `partialJson`, `arguments`, and `thinking` at 12k characters before the larger byte-budget logic ran.

Raising the char cap alone fixes premature truncation for long ASCII replies, but it leaves a second failure mode: long UTF-8-heavy text can bypass the char cap and then be replaced entirely by the oversized placeholder during the later byte-budget pass.

This update fixes both paths:

- long replies that still fit the budget remain visible
- byte-heavy replies are truncated to a readable prefix instead of being dropped completely

## Test

```bash
pnpm -C /tmp/openclaw-pr-53243 exec vitest run --config vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
```

Fixes #53242

target.title: fix(irc): prevent silent send failures and fix NickServ nick reclaim
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes multiple issues causing IRC messages to fail silently and NickServ nick reclamation to not work when connecting with a fallback nick.

## Changes

### Silent send failures
1. **`sendPrivmsg` throws on empty sanitized text** — previously returned silently, so callers never knew the message was dropped.
2. **`sendRaw` checks for closed/destroyed socket** — prevents silent writes to dead connections.
3. **Transient client drain delay** — adds 500ms wait between sending PRIVMSG and calling `quit()` on throwaway connections. Without this, the QUIT could race ahead of the message on the wire.

### NickServ nick reclamation
4. **`IDENTIFY <account> <password>` format** — when connected with a fallback nick (e.g. `mrpink_`), NickServ needs the account name explicitly. Previously sent just `IDENTIFY <password>`, which fails because the fallback nick isn't a registered account.
5. **Post-IDENTIFY GHOST + NICK** — after identifying, GHOSTs any zombie holding the desired nick, then sends `NICK` to reclaim. Handles both reserved-nick (Ergo enforce-nick) and zombie session cases.

## Testing
- All 53 existing IRC tests pass
- 1 new test added for `IDENTIFY <account> <password>` format
- All lints and checks clean
- Manually tested against Ergo IRCd — confirmed nick reclaim works end-to-end

target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.

target.title: fix(acp): time out stuck session lane tasks
target.body:
## Problem

When an ACP session gets stuck (e.g. Gemini CLI `session/load` fails), it holds the session lane forever, blocking all subsequent tasks from dequeuing and executing.

## Solution

Add a configurable `taskTimeoutMs` to the session lane. When a task exceeds this timeout:

1. The task is marked as failed with an `AcpRuntimeError`
2. The lane is released
3. The next queued task is automatically dequeued and executed

### Configuration

```yaml
acp:
  sessionLane:
    taskTimeoutMs: 600000  # default: 10 minutes
```

### Changes

- `src/acp/control-plane/manager.ts`: Add timeout timer on task start, clear on completion, auto-release lane on expiry
- `src/config/types.acp.ts`: Add `acp.sessionLane.taskTimeoutMs` config with schema validation
- `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`: Add regression test covering stuck-task-timeout → next-task-dequeue flow

### Testing

- All 37 existing + new tests pass
- `pnpm check:base-config-schema` passes
- Build failure is pre-existing on upstream/main (missing LINE provider files), unrelated to this change

*Resubmitted after closing #33884 to stay under 10-PR limit. Previously #52628.*

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ACP session lane work no longer stays wedged forever when a backend session creation call hangs, such as a Gemini CLI ACP session/load call that never returns.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, commit \`09aded5a34c1\`, Node 22 via pnpm. The command imported the real \`AcpSessionManager\` and \`SessionActorQueue\` from this branch and used in-memory session metadata plus a local ACP runtime adapter shim whose first \`ensureSession\` intentionally never resolves to reproduce the non-returning backend load condition without credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran \`pnpm exec tsx\` with an inline local harness from the repo root. The harness starts two same-session \`initializeSession\` calls against the real ACP control plane, first with \`acp.sessionLane.taskTimeoutMs: 0\` and then with \`acp.sessionLane.taskTimeoutMs: 100\`, while the first adapter \`ensureSession\` ignores abort and completes late.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the local run:

\`\`\`text
proof_start=2026-05-10T05:38:32.042Z
surface=real AcpSessionManager + SessionActorQueue with fake ACP adapter simulating Gemini CLI session/load hang
before_timeout_config taskTimeoutMs=0 ensure_calls=1 first=pending second=pending lane_queue_depth=2 active_runtime_sessions=0 metadata_upserts=0
after_timeout_config taskTimeoutMs=100 ensure_calls=2 first=rejected first_error="ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: ACP session operation timed out. Please retry." first_signal_aborted=true signal_events=["ensure#1:signal_aborted"] second=resolved second_runtime=proof-runtime-2 metadata_upserts=1 active_runtime_sessions=1
late_stale_completion metadata_upserts=1 persisted_runtime=proof-runtime-2 close_events=["init-meta-failed:proof-runtime-1-late-stale"] active_runtime_sessions=1
proof_end=2026-05-10T05:38:32.575Z
\`\`\`

- **Observed result after fix**: With timeout disabled, the first hung load keeps the lane occupied and the second same-session init remains pending. With \`taskTimeoutMs=100\`, the hung first init rejects with \`ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED\`, the runtime signal is aborted, the next queued init runs and persists \`proof-runtime-2\`, and the late stale completion does not overwrite metadata or clear the active runtime cache.
- **What was not tested**: A live Gemini account-backed CLI process was not used; the local adapter shim reproduced the same non-returning ACP \`ensureSession\`/session-load behavior so the control-plane timeout, abort, queue release, and stale fencing paths were exercised deterministically.

target.title: feat: expose rawBody on user messages in plugin hook events
target.body:
### Summary

Add `rawBody` field to user messages in `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`, and `before_agent_start` (legacy) hook events. This provides plugins with the original user input before channel structural context, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` metadata, and prompt-build hook injections are prepended.

### Motivation

Plugin hooks receive `event.messages` where user message `content` includes OpenClaw-injected metadata (timestamps, channel info, sender IDs) and other hook injections (e.g., memory context). Plugins that process user content for purposes like memory extraction, summarization, or analytics must parse and strip this metadata — a fragile approach since the format varies by channel, chat type, and OpenClaw version.

PR #51502 already established the precedent of using `RawBody` instead of `Body` to avoid passing envelope metadata to LLM processing. This PR extends that principle to plugin hook events.

### Changes

- Compute `rawBodyForHooks` from `CommandBody ?? RawBody ?? Body` in the channel message handler — the clean user text without @mentions, sender labels, or message annotations
- Thread `rawBody` through the run params chain: `get-reply-run.ts` → `runReplyAgent` → `runAgentTurnWithFallback` → `runEmbeddedPiAgent` → `runEmbeddedAttempt`
- Include `rawBody` in hook event objects for `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end`
- Add `rawBody` to `FollowupRun` type so the value survives queueing/deferred runs
- `rawBody` is `undefined` for non-channel runs (CLI, heartbeat, cron)

### Backward Compatibility

- Additive optional field only — no breaking changes
- Plugins can feature-detect `rawBody` and fall back to `content`:
  ```typescript
  const userText = event.rawBody ?? extractText(lastUser.content);
  ```
- Plugin SDK API baseline unchanged (no drift)

### Use Case

Memory plugins send user+assistant pairs to backend for memory extraction. Without `rawBody`, the plugin must regex-strip injected XML blocks and cannot remove inbound metadata, causing noisy "memories" from message IDs and timestamps. With `rawBody`, the plugin gets clean user input directly.

target.title: 🛡️ [SECURITY] Zero-Dep Runtime Shield — Circuit Breaker, Anomaly Detection & 4 Critical Vuln Patches
target.body:
## 🛡️ OpenClaw Shield — Runtime Security Hardening Layer

> **50+ tests · Zero dependencies · Pure functions · Drop-in integration**

---

### 🚨 Critical Security Patches

| Vulnerability | Severity | Fix |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| **Command Injection** in `config.openFile` | 🔴 Critical | `exec()` → `execFile()` to block shell injection via config path |
| **Unsafe `eval()` bypass** in browser evaluate | 🔴 Critical | Blocked pattern filter: `__proto__`, `constructor[`, `import()`, `require()`, `process.env`, `child_process` |
| **Loopback rate-limit bypass** | 🟠 High | Enforce 10× throttled limit for localhost (was fully exempt) — prevents local brute-force |
| **Missing security headers** | 🟡 Medium | `X-Frame-Options`, `COOP`, `CORP`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy` |

---

### 🧱 New Shield Modules (`src/security/shield/`)

| Module | Purpose |
|:-------|:--------|
| `circuit-breaker.ts` | 3-state circuit breaker (CLOSED → HALF_OPEN → OPEN) with exponential backoff |
| `session-monitor.ts` | 6 deterministic anomaly rules: auth flood, brute force, impossible travel, device spray, global flood, failure spike |
| `function-health.ts` | Per-function health scoring (0–100) with baseline learning |
| `function-throttle.ts` | Progressive rate limiting driven by health score |
| `emergency-escalation.ts` | Multi-function failure detection with graduated response (ALERT → PARTIAL_PAUSE → TOTAL_HALT) |
| `metrics-collector.ts` | Per-minute request aggregation with percentile calculation |
| `webhook-dispatch.ts` | HMAC-SHA256 signed security notifications with retry logic |

target.title: Embedded: stop replaying historical session replies
target.body:
## Summary

This prevents embedded subscribe from replaying historical assistant replies when attaching to an existing session.

## Problem

When `subscribeEmbeddedPiSession(...)` subscribes to a session that already contains assistant messages, the initial replay can include historical assistant lifecycle events. Those events currently flow through the same outward-facing assistant delivery path as new replies.

That means a subscriber attaching to an existing session can emit an old assistant final again, even though it was already delivered before subscription.

## Fix

Track whether embedded subscribe is still processing the initial replay and keep a set of preexisting session messages. While initial replay is in progress, and for messages already present when subscription started, assistant lifecycle handlers now ignore those events instead of treating them like fresh outward replies.

This keeps historical transcript state intact while preventing duplicate outward delivery.

## Tests

Add a regression test that subscribes to an existing session containing a historical assistant reply, replays those historical events, then emits a new assistant reply. The subscriber should only emit the new reply.

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-pi-session.subscribeembeddedpisession.test.ts -t "does not replay historical assistant finals when subscribing to an existing session"`

## Note

This PR is intentionally separate from the transcript-only `gateway-injected` filtering fix. Both touch embedded subscribe delivery, but they address different duplicate-reply failure modes.
